title: Displaying data from item updates
excerpt:
I want to display information from my item updates but would be available outside the updates section, perhaps as a view. 
It would be similar to the list that appears within the updates. 
Is this possible in some way?

posts.author: celeste21 (celeste)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to display information from my item updates but would be available outside the updates section, perhaps as a view.

It would be similar to the list that appears within the updates.

Is this possible in some way?

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Celeste!

When you mention the list in the Updates Section, are you meaning the individual updates that you can scroll through?

Are you looking to display just certain columns from your items? Perhaps you could use the Cards View to show the item information in a similar fashion? What do you think?

I’d love to hear a little bit more about your use case and what you would like to see so I can suggest a solution that works for you!

posts.author: celeste21 (celeste)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks @CharlotteK .
Yes it is the scrolling itemised updates I would like to display more easily.

We use updates to track emails, calls etc with each stakeholder. It works well for us because we can email dierectly in to create an update, rather than add an update later (and perhaps forget).

But for infrequent users we would like easier access to the ‘hisotry’ for a stakeholder.

I think it would be easy for these users to > find the view > filter for the person > scroll through the history of interactions.

At present they need to find the stakeholder, find the updates button (some people find this unclear) then find the right veiw to scroll through the history, then open them to see the full text (e.g. an email trail).

I hope this makes sense…

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @celeste21 ,
What about Board updates widget? Doesn’t it work for you?

posts.author: IsaacWilliams (Isaac Williams)
posts.cookedHtml:
I figured out a way to accomplish this without a marketplace app. You just have to approach the problem backwards. Instead of populating a column with input from your updates, create an update when your team updates a column.

  1. Create a text column to your board and label it something like ‘Update/Next Steps’.
  2. Create a new automation to create an update when the column changes.
  3. ‘When this happens’ = “column changes”.
  4. Click the ‘column’ placeholder and select your ‘Update/Next Steps’ column.
  5. ‘Then do this’ = “create update”.
  6. Click the ‘this text’ placeholder and use the board items building blocks to automatically pull in the latest text input from ‘Update/Next Steps’ as well as any additional information you might want.
  7. The update will be created by the Automation user, so I use something like this in the 'this text 'message to identify the user making the update:
    {user name} added an update to {project’s Name}.
    Project Status: {project’s Project Status}
    Latest Update: {project’s Update/Next Steps}

You can use the Board Updates Widget to review all the latest updates from your board, but the method I’ve laid out allows you to easily view the latest updates from your team on every project without having to click into each item, while keeping record of previous updates in the item update feed.

Hope this helps!
